SEARCH
Package com.nib.commons.storage

Class NChar

java.lang.Object
com.nib.commons.storage.AbstractNField
com.nib.commons.storage.AbstractNElementary
com.nib.commons.storage.NChar
All Implemented Interfaces:
IDataArea, Cloneable
Direct Known Subclasses:
GetMainStorageId, NCharBin, NCharInspected, SpecialRegister
public class NChar extends AbstractNElementary
Represents a character-based data structure that provides functionality for handling string, byte, and character values. This class supports operations like initialization, setting and getting values, comparisons, and alignment. It extends the functionality provided by the AbstractNElementary class.

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested classes/interfaces inherited from class com.nib.commons.storage.AbstractNField

    AbstractNField.Justified

    Compares the current object's stored value against the given integer value after formatting it with leading zeroes to match the required length.

    Compares the current StringValue with the given StringValue and determines if the current value is lexicographically less than the provided value.

    lessThan(String value)
    Compares the input string with the current object to determine if it is less than the stored value.

  • Constructor Details

    • NChar

      public NChar(Memory memory, int offset, int length)
      Constructs an NChar object with the specified memory, offset, and length.
      Parameters:
      memory - the memory object where this NChar is stored
      offset - the offset in the memory for this NChar
      length - the length of this NChar in memory

    • NChar

      public NChar(int length)
      Constructs an NChar object with the specified length. This instance is not yet associated with any memory or offset, and it initializes with the given length.
      Parameters:
      length - the length of the NChar field

    • justified

      public NChar justified(AbstractNField.Justified j)
      Description copied from class: AbstractNField
      Sets the justification value for this field.

      Overrides:
      justified in class AbstractNField
      Parameters:
      j - the justification alignment to apply to the field
      Returns:
      the current instance of AbstractNField for method chaining.

      Description copied from class: AbstractNField

    • resetDefaultValue

      protected void resetDefaultValue()
      Description copied from class: AbstractNElementary
      Resets the field to its default value. Implementations of this method should define the specific logic required to restore the field to a predefined state or value. This method is typically invoked during memory allocation or field reinitialization processes.
      Specified by:
      resetDefaultValue in class AbstractNElementary

    • setValue

      public void setValue(StringValue value)
      Sets the value of this NChar instance using the specified StringValue.
      Parameters:
      value - the StringValue to set as the value

    • setValue

      public void setValue(char value)
      Sets the value of this NChar instance to the specified char value.
      Parameters:
      value - the character value to be set for this NChar instance

    • setValue

      public void setValue(char[] value)
      Sets the value of this NChar instance to the specified character array.
      Parameters:
      value - the character array to be set for this NChar instance

    • setValue

      public void setValue(byte[] value)
      Sets the value of this NChar instance using the specified byte array.
      Parameters:
      value - the byte array to be set as the value for this NChar instance

    • setValue

      public void setValue(NZoned field)
      Sets the value of this NChar instance using the specified NZoned field. The contents of the given NZoned field are moved into this NChar instance, and the value is padded to match the length of this instance if necessary.
      Parameters:
      field - the NZoned field whose value will be set into this NChar instance

    • setValue

      public void setValue(NPacked field)
      Sets the value of this NChar instance using the specified NPacked field. The value from the provided NPacked field is converted to its absolute string representation and set to this instance.
      Parameters:
      field - the NPacked field whose value will be used to set this NChar instance

    • setValue

      public void setValue(NGroup field)
      Sets the value of this NChar instance using the specified NGroup field. The contents of the given NGroup field are moved into this NChar instance, and the value is padded to match the length of this instance if necessary.
      Parameters:
      field - the NGroup field whose value will be set into this NChar instance

    • setValue

      public void setValue(NChar field)
      Sets the value of this NChar instance using the specified NChar field. The contents of the given NChar field are moved into this instance, and the value is padded as necessary to match the length of this NChar instance.
      Parameters:
      field - the NChar field whose value will be set into this NChar instance

    • setValue

      public void setValue(NInteger field)
      Sets the value of this NChar instance using the specified NInteger field. The value of the given NInteger field is formatted and set to this NChar instance. The value is zero-padded to match the number of digits specified in the NInteger field.
      Parameters:
      field - the NInteger field whose value is to be set into this NChar instance

    • setValue

      public void setValue(int value)
      Sets the value of this NChar instance to the specified integer value. The integer value is aligned to the left and padded with spaces.
      Parameters:
      value - the integer value to be set for this NChar instance

    • unstring

      public void unstring(SpecialRegister delimiter, NChar... into)
      Splits a value using the specified delimiter and assigns the resulting parts into the provided NChar objects.
      Parameters:
      delimiter - the delimiter represented as a SpecialRegister, used to split the value
      into - the array of NChar objects to store the parts after splitting

    • unstring

      public void unstring(char delimiter, NChar... into)
      Splits the current string representation of the value using the specified delimiter and assigns the resulting parts into the provided NChar objects.
      Parameters:
      delimiter - the character used as a delimiter to split the string value
      into - an array of NChar objects where the resulting parts of the split operation will be stored

    • unstring

      public void unstring(byte delimiter, NChar... into)
      Splits the current string representation of the object based on the specified delimiter and assigns the resulting substrings into the provided NChar objects.
      Parameters:
      delimiter - The byte value used as the delimiter for splitting the string. Special values include: - `Space`: Splits by whitespace. - `LowValue`: Splits by null character ("0x00"). - `HighValue`: Splits by high-value character ("0xff"). - Any other byte delimiter will be converted into a character for splitting.
      into - A variable-length array of NChar objects where the split substrings will be stored. The number of substrings assigned is limited by the size of `into` or the number of resulting tokens, whichever is smaller.

    • equals

      public boolean equals(String value)
      Compares the specified string with the string returned by the getString method to determine if they are equal.
      Parameters:
      value - the string to be compared with the string from getString. If the specified string is null, this method returns false.
      Returns:
      true if the specified string is equal to the result of getString; false otherwise.

    • equals

      public boolean equals(byte[] value)
      Compares the specified byte array with the current object to determine if they are equal.
      Parameters:
      value - the byte array to compare with the current object
      Returns:
      true if the specified byte array is equal to the current object, false otherwise

    • equals

      public boolean equals(StringValue value)
      Compares this object to the specified StringValue for equality.
      Parameters:
      value - the StringValue object to be compared
      Returns:
      true if the specified object is equal to this object; false otherwise

    • notEquals

      public boolean notEquals(String value)
      Compares the provided string value with the string value of the current instance and determines if they are not equal.
      Parameters:
      value - the string value to compare with the current instance
      Returns:
      true if the provided value is not equal to the current instance's string value or if the provided value is null; false otherwise

      public boolean notEquals(StringValue value)
      Compares the current object with the given StringValue and determines if they are not equal.
      Parameters:
      value - the StringValue object to compare with the current object
      Returns:
      true if the current object and the given StringValue are not equal, false otherwise

      Compares the given string value to determine if it is greater than the current instance.

      Returns:
      true if the current instance is greater than the given string value, false otherwise

      Returns:

      Returns:
      true if the input string is less than the stored value; false otherwise

      Compares the given string value to determine if it is greater than or equal to the current object based on specific comparison rules.
      Parameters:
      value - the string value to compare against
      Returns:
      true if the current object is greater than or equal to the given value, false otherwise

      Returns:
      true if the current object's value is greater than or equal to the value of the provided StringValue object; false otherwise

      Returns:
      true if the current object is less than or equal to the input value, false otherwise

      Returns:

      public boolean equals(int intValue)
      Compares the given integer value, formatted with leading zeroes to match the length of the current object's expected value, with the object's internal value.
      Parameters:

      Returns:
      true if the formatted integer value matches the object's internal value, false otherwise.

      Returns:

      public boolean lessEqualThan(int value)
      Compares the given integer value, formatted to match the length of the current object, with the current object's value and checks if it is less than or equal to it.
      Parameters:
      value - the integer value to be compared
      Returns:
      true if the formatted integer value is less than or equal to the current object's value; false otherwise

    • inRange

      public boolean inRange(String floorValue, String ceilValue)
      Determines if the current value is within the range specified by the provided floor and ceiling values.
      Parameters:
      floorValue - the lower bound of the range to compare against, represented as a string
      ceilValue - the upper bound of the range to compare against, represented as a string
      Returns:
      true if the current value is greater than or equal to the floorValue and less than or equal to the ceilValue; false otherwise
